front: Short Story: ends 'Romance at short notice was her specialty' after Vera tricks Framton Nuttel into fleeing when three hunters return through the title structure
Short Story: Vera explains Nuttel's sudden departure as due to a 'horror of dogs'
Short Story: boy named Conradin worships a polecat-ferret that kills his guardian Mrs. De Ropp; he calmly makes toast as the story ends
Short Story: boy steals powdered nutmeg as a reward for causing a toothache
Short Story: feuding men Znaeym and von Gradwitz trapped under a fallen tree in the Carpathians reconcile before the one-word ending 'Wolves.'
Short Story: Cornelius Appin teaches the title cat to speak; it reveals owners' secrets at Mrs. Blemley's party
Short Story: Nicholas puts a frog in his breakfast and discovers a stag-hunt tapestry; refuses to rescue his aunt from a rainwater tank, calling her the Devil
Short Story: a woman pretending to be a governess makes children reenact the Rape of the Sabine Women; pretends to keep a leopard cub in her luggage
Short Story: Clovis pretends to be a bishop's secretary plotting a massacre to cure J. P. Huddle's addiction to routine
Author: pen name from the Rubaiyat's word for 'cupbearer'; recurring characters include Clovis Sangrail and Reginald
Author: early career included reworking Alice in Wonderland into political satires for the Westminster Gazette
